name: product_problem_definition
content: A strong product decision starts with a clearly defined user problem, target audience, and success criteria.

name: product_prioritization_tradeoffs
content: Prioritization should balance impact, urgency, implementation cost, and learning value rather than stakeholder volume alone.

name: product_mvp_scope
content: A good MVP is the smallest version that can validate value for a real user in a real context.

name: product_user_journey
content: Mapping the user journey helps identify key entry points, friction, expectations, and moments of delight.

name: product_feedback_loop
content: Short feedback loops improve product quality faster than large speculative planning cycles.

name: product_metrics_alignment
content: Metrics are useful only when they align with the underlying user value the product is supposed to deliver.

name: product_execution_plan
content: Execution plans should make scope, owners, dependencies, risks, and review checkpoints explicit.

name: product_postmortem_learning
content: A useful postmortem focuses on facts, decisions, assumptions, and concrete improvements instead of blame.
